'''Aaron Aspin Heremaia'''<ref>{{cite book
|last1 = Coffey
|first1 = John
|last2 = Wood
|first2 = Bernie
|title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
|publisher =Huia Publishers
|year = 2008
|location = Wellington, New Zealand
|pages = 334
|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
|doi =
|id =
|isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}}</ref> (born {{birth date|df=yes|1982|9|19}}<ref name=hullfcbio>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/223862/aaron-heremaia|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131204210044/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/223862/aaron-heremaia|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 December 2013|title=Official website of Hull FC: Profile of Aaron Heremaia|access-date=22 August 2012}}</ref>) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|hb}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Heremaia was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.<ref name="hullfcbio"/>

==Playing career==
Educated at [[Tangaroa College]] and a [[Manurewa Marlins]] junior, Heremaia started his career in 2001 playing for the [[Hibiscus Coast Raiders]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]] whilst on a [[New Zealand Warriors]] development contract.<ref name="NZ_Herald_10567182">{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10567182|title=NRL: Heremaia back in the fold|author=Jessup, Peter|date=18 April 2009|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=15 September 2011}}</ref> where he played under successful coaches [[Tony Benson (rugby league)|Tony Benson]] and [[Brian McClennan]]. Heremaia however failed to be picked up by the Warriors.<ref name="NZ_Herald_10567182" /> In 2004 Heremaia moved to Australia to play in the [[NSWRL Premier League]], where he represented the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] and [[North Sydney Bears]] in successive years.{{fact|date=April 2022}}

In 2006 he moved to England to play for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Co-operative Championship|National League One]], again linking up with Tony Benson. That year Heremaia was part of the team that won the [[Northern Rail Cup]]. He has also conditionally signed but never played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in 2007 and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/league-ex-junior-kiwi-walks-out-on-uk-club/4DRA7UF7H3375GJQMR3AGQEK2Y/ |title=League: Ex-Junior Kiwi walks out on UK club |publisher=New Zealand Herald |date=8 January 2008 |access-date=25 September 2021}}</ref>

Returning to New Zealand and the [[Manurewa Marlins]], Heremaia was able to make the [[Auckland Vulcans]] team for 2008, playing in the [[NSW Cup]]. From here he was able to impress the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and was signed to a contract for the 2009 season. He made his [[National Rugby League|first grade]] début in Round Twelve against the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/217/locke-and-heremaia-to-make-nrl-debuts Locke and Heremaia to make NRL debuts] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090529063940/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/217/locke-and-heremaia-to-make-nrl-debuts |date=2009-05-29}} ''Warriors.co.nz'', 26 May 2009</ref> He was re-signed by the Warriors until the end of the 2011 season.<ref>[http://home.nzcity.co.nz/news/article.aspx?id=99936&cat=989&fm=newsmain,narts Patrick Ah Van, Aaron Heremaia and Isaac John have re-signed with the warriors until the end of the 2011 season] ''www.rugbyleague.co.nz'', 10 May 2009</ref> Heremaia joined [[Hull F.C.]] in 2012 on a two-year deal with an option for a third.<ref>[http://www.code13rugbyleague.com/2011/06/02/hull-fc-sign-new-zealand-warrior-aaron-heremaia/ Hull FC sign New Zealand Warrior Aaron Heremaia] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724171453/http://www.code13rugbyleague.com/2011/06/02/hull-fc-sign-new-zealand-warrior-aaron-heremaia/|date=24 July 2011}} ''code13rugbyleague.com'', 2 June 2011</ref>

On 8 May 2014, [[Hull F.C.]] announced that they would release Heremaia at the end of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/9302836/super-league-hull-hooker-aaron-heremaia-not-offered-new-deal|title=Super League: Hull hooker Aaron Heremaia not offered new deal|work=Sky Sports}}</ref>

One day after Hull announced Aaron would leave at the end of the season, the [[Widnes Vikings]] announced he will arrive at the club at the end of the 2014 season to play in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/9303562/super-league-widnes-vikings-sign-aaron-heremaia-after-hull-release|title=Super League: Widnes Vikings sign Aaron Heremaia after Hull release|work=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/30167/heremaia-to-join-the-vikings|title=Heremaia to join the Vikings for 2015|work=2013.superleague.co.uk}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Heremaia played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2001. He made his début for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] side in 2002.<ref>{{cite book
|last1 = Coffey
|first1 = John
|last2 = Wood
|first2 = Bernie
|title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
|publisher = Huia Publishers
|year = 2008
|location = Wellington, New Zealand
|pages = 326
|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
|doi =
|id =
|isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}}</ref> He regularly appeared for the side while based in New Zealand and captained New Zealand Māori on several occasions.

For the [[2010 Anzac Test]], Heremaia was selected to play for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] from the interchange bench in their loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].
